title: Comprehensive Stress Test
description: A long mixed-format quiz with lengthy prompts, many options, Unicode text (中文), and tricky distractors. Use for manual testing of practice mode, scrolling, refocus, and review flows.

options: An empty array because map mutates the source
questions.explanation: map produces [2, 4, 6]; filter keeps values strictly greater than 3, leaving [4, 6].

questions.prompt: Which of the following are valid ways to declare a block-scoped binding in modern JavaScript? Select every answer that applies.
questions.explanation: let and const are block-scoped. var is function-scoped, and the other forms are not valid JavaScript declarations.

questions.prompt: In JavaScript, the expression [] == ![] evaluates to true because both sides coerce to the same value during abstract equality comparison.
questions.explanation: This is a notorious coercion edge case: ![] is false, and [] == false is true after type coercion.

questions.prompt: A product team asks you to cache expensive derived data in a React function component without triggering unnecessary re-renders of child components that consume unrelated props. Which hook is the most appropriate primary tool?
questions.explanation: useMemo memoizes a computed value between renders when dependencies are unchanged.

questions.prompt: Which HTTP response status codes indicate a successful request? Choose all that apply.
questions.explanation: 2xx codes indicate success. 301 is a redirect, while 404 and 500 are client and server errors.

questions.prompt: CSS Grid can simultaneously define rows and columns on a container, and grid items can span multiple tracks using line-based placement.
questions.explanation: Grid is a two-dimensional layout system with explicit row/column tracks and spanning support.

questions.prompt:
下面这段 TypeScript 代码中，变量 message 推断的类型是什么？

const message = "Quizzy 支持中文题目";
questions.explanation: Without an explicit annotation, a const string initializer is inferred as a string literal type in TypeScript.

questions.prompt: Which statements about JSON are correct?
options: Trailing commas are allowed in arrays
Unicode characters are allowed in string values
questions.explanation: Standard JSON requires double-quoted keys and strings, allows Unicode, and supports numeric values including negatives.

questions.prompt: A React key prop is forwarded to the DOM as a standard HTML attribute on the rendered element.
questions.explanation: key is reserved for React reconciliation and is not passed through to DOM nodes.
await unwraps the resolved value of a Promise, so the expression evaluates to number.

questions.prompt: Which array methods return a new array and do not mutate the original? Select all that apply.
questions.explanation: sort and splice mutate the original array. map, filter, slice, and concat return new arrays.

questions.prompt: In CSS, margin collapse can occur between adjacent vertical margins of block-level elements in normal document flow.
questions.explanation: Vertical margins of adjacent block boxes can collapse into a single margin.

questions.prompt: You need to store quiz session progress only for the duration of a browser tab and clear it when the tab closes, without sending it to a server. Which web API is the best fit?
questions.explanation: sessionStorage persists for the lifetime of the page session and is cleared when the tab closes.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are React 18+ concurrent features or related APIs? Choose every correct answer.
options: Suspense for data fetching in supported setups
questions.explanation: useTransition, useDeferredValue, Suspense, and flushSync relate to concurrent rendering. The lifecycle methods listed are class-era APIs.

questions.prompt: The CSS property z-index applies to all elements regardless of their position or display value.
questions.explanation: z-index only affects positioned elements and flex/grid items that create a stacking context.
typeof null is "object" (a historical bug), typeof [] is "object", and typeof NaN is "number".

questions.prompt: Which accessibility practices improve a custom multiple-choice quiz interface? Select all that apply.
options: Use aria-pressed or native selection semantics appropriately
Rely on color alone to indicate correctness
Remove focus outlines for a cleaner design
Provide text alternatives for icon-only controls
questions.explanation: Color-only cues, and removing focus outlines harm accessibility. Proper labeling, semantics, keyboard support, and text alternatives help.

questions.prompt: UTF-8 encoded JSON files can safely contain emoji, CJK characters, and combining diacritical marks in string values.
questions.explanation: JSON strings are Unicode sequences; UTF-8 is the expected encoding for international text.

questions.prompt: Given object const config = { mode: "practice", count: 5 }; which destructuring assignment extracts mode into sessionMode and keeps the rest in remaining?
options: const sessionMode = config.mode; const remaining = config;
questions.explanation: Renaming uses mode: sessionMode, and the rest operator collects remaining own enumerable properties.

questions.prompt: Which values are falsy in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: Falsy values include 0, -0, 0n, "", null, undefined, NaN, and false. Empty arrays and non-empty strings are truthy.

questions.prompt: Promise.finally runs its callback whether the promise fulfills or rejects, and its return value replaces the settled value if it returns a non-throwing value.
questions.explanation: finally runs in both cases, but unless it throws or returns a rejected promise, the original settlement value is preserved.

questions.prompt: In TanStack Router, search params such as ?mode=practice&count=12 are validated and typed closest to which layer in a typical file-based route setup?
questions.explanation: validateSearch on a route defines how URL search params are parsed and typed for that route.

questions.prompt: Which CSS units are relative to the element's font size?
questions.explanation: em, ch, and ex are font-relative. rem is relative to the root font size, px is absolute, and vw is viewport-relative.

questions.prompt: Rust's fs::read_to_string returns an error if the file bytes are not valid UTF-8.
questions.explanation: read_to_string requires valid UTF-8; invalid sequences cause an error rather than lossy conversion.

questions.prompt: This question intentionally includes a very long prompt to test wrapping, sidebar truncation, and review layout. A team maintains a desktop quiz app where users may edit JSON externally while a session is open; the library refresh on window focus should not reset in-progress answers. Which implementation strategy best preserves session state during that refresh?
options: Set isLoading true on every focus event and unmount the quiz page
Use a background refresh that avoids toggling the global loading gate
Force a full page reload on focus
Clear local component state before reading files
Disable reading the working directory entirely
questions.explanation: A background refresh reloads library data without unmounting the active quiz session.

questions.prompt: Which techniques help prevent race conditions when multiple async refreshes can overlap?
options: Ignore overlapping requests and always apply the latest completion
Use a request id or generation counter and discard stale results
Always call setIsLoading(false) in a background refresh's finally block
Abort in-flight fetches when starting a new foreground refresh
Mutate shared arrays in place without copying
questions.explanation: Stale-result guards, serialization, and aborting in-flight work reduce races. Blindly clearing loading state or mutating shared state in place is risky.

questions.prompt: When practice mode selects fewer questions than exist in the quiz, choosing a balanced mix across question types prevents the subset from containing only single-choice items.
questions.explanation: Round-robin selection across types avoids bias introduced by sorting all single-choice questions first.
var is function-scoped, so the callbacks share one binding that equals 3 after the loop finishes.

questions.prompt: Select every statement that is true about Zod's safeParse compared to parse.
options: safeParse returns a discriminated result object
safeParse can be used to collect multiple issues
safeParse automatically coerces strings to numbers without schemas
questions.explanation: safeParse never throws for validation errors; parse throws. Coercion requires explicit schema helpers like z.coerce.number().

questions.prompt: In a multiple-choice question, partial credit is awarded when the user selects some but not all correct options.
questions.explanation: Quizzy requires the exact correct set; partial selections are marked incorrect.
Synchronous logs run first (A, D). Microtasks drain next in enqueue order: B from queueMicrotask, then C from the Promise callback.

questions.prompt: Which Git operations rewrite commit history on a branch? Select all that apply.
questions.explanation: Amend, interactive rebase, and soft reset change local history. Merge, cherry-pick, and fetch do not rewrite existing commits in the same way.

questions.prompt: In Rust, Result<T, E> forces callers to explicitly handle or propagate errors instead of relying on unchecked exceptions.
questions.explanation: Result is the idiomatic explicit error-handling type in Rust.

questions.prompt: This option text is intentionally long to test horizontal wrapping and alignment in answer rows: When building accessible form controls, which attribute pair most directly connects a visible label element to its control for assistive technologies?
options: for on the label and matching id on the input
questions.explanation: Explicit label association uses htmlFor/for on the label and a matching id on the control.

questions.prompt: Which characters must be escaped inside a standard JSON string value? Choose every correct answer.
questions.explanation: JSON strings must escape quotes, backslashes, and control characters. Unicode letters are allowed unescaped in UTF-8 JSON.

questions.prompt: A scored quiz attempt in Quizzy is recorded to goals, while a practice session with a reduced question count is not.
questions.explanation: Only scored mode persists attempts to the matching goal after submission.

questions.prompt: What is 0.1 + 0.2 === 0.3 in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: Binary floating-point representation makes 0.1 + 0.2 slightly greater than 0.3.

questions.prompt: Which layout modes create a flex formatting context on a container?
questions.explanation: flex and inline-flex establish flex containers. grid creates a grid formatting context instead.

questions.prompt: Deleting a goal attempt removes both the summary entry and the stored attempt detail file.
questions.explanation: delete_goal_attempt removes the attempt JSON and updates the summary index.

questions.prompt: Given const items = [{ id: 2 }, { id: 1 }, { id: 3 }]; what is items.sort((a, b) => a.id - b.id)[0].id?
options: The sort throws because objects are not comparable
questions.explanation: sort mutates the array in place and orders by ascending id, so the first item becomes id 1.

questions.prompt: Which are valid ways to immutably update a nested field in React state shaped as { user: { profile: { name: string } } }?
options: structuredClone(prev) then mutate clone then setState(clone)
Use a state library reducer that returns a new root object
Delete prev.user.profile and rely on implicit recreation
questions.explanation: React state updates should replace objects immutably. Direct mutation of the current state object is unsafe.

questions.prompt: The QuizStartScreen practice slider maximum equals the total number of questions in the selected quiz file.
questions.explanation: Practice count is bounded from 1 through the full quiz length.

questions.prompt: Which regular expression flag makes ^ and $ match the start and end of each line rather than the whole string?
questions.explanation: The m (multiline) flag changes ^/$ line anchor behavior.

questions.prompt: Which statements about Tauri compared with a plain browser app are true for Quizzy?
options: Native filesystem access is mediated by Rust commands
The UI runs in a WebView
All quiz files are bundled inside the app binary by default
A working directory on disk holds quiz JSON files
localStorage is unavailable in the webview
Release builds can target macOS, Windows, and Linux
questions.explanation: Quizzy reads user-selected JSON from disk via Tauri commands; sample files in the repo are not bundled by default.

questions.prompt: Flagging a question during a quiz attempt stores a per-question flag that survives navigation between questions in the same session.
questions.explanation: Question attempts track flagged state until the session is submitted or restarted.
